history_exam.label: Initial symptom onset
history_exam.detail: Well until 11 days before admission when dizziness and right temporal headache developed

history_exam.label: Headache course
history_exam.detail: Right temporal headaches recurred after transient improvement; worse with noise

history_exam.label: Worsening prior to admission
history_exam.detail: Three days before admission headache severity increased significantly

history_exam.label: Associated chest symptom
history_exam.detail: Pleuritic chest pain on the left developed the day after worsening headache

history_exam.label: Gastrointestinal symptoms
history_exam.detail: Emesis occurred the day after headache worsened; later awoke with nausea and vomiting the following morning

history_exam.label: Ocular prodrome
history_exam.detail: While in outside ED both eyes felt 'weird' but no diplopia or changes in vision initially

history_exam.label: Response to ED meds
history_exam.detail: Prochlorperazine and diphenhydramine administered in outside ED with partial improvement

history_exam.label: Antibiotic given at discharge from outside ED
history_exam.detail: Discharged on azithromycin

history_exam.label: Treatment by primary care after ED visit
history_exam.detail: Seen by PCP after nausea/vomiting; received trimethobenzamide and IV fluids

history_exam.label: Acute ocular worsening
history_exam.detail: That evening bilateral eye pain developed and over ~3 hours periorbital swelling and right-eye proptosis occurred

history_exam.label: Onset of diplopia
history_exam.detail: Diplopia noted 5 hours after onset of eye pain

history_exam.label: Progression and transfer
history_exam.detail: Returned to ED and was transferred to tertiary care hospital for further evaluation

history_exam.label: Facial/jaw pain during admission
history_exam.detail: Right-sided facial pain persisted and moved to lateral jaw and neck on the right, associated with trismus

history_exam.label: Chronic medical history
history_exam.detail: History of temporomandibular joint disorder

history_exam.label: Chronic medication
history_exam.detail: Oral contraceptive pills

history_exam.label: Medications administered in outside ED
history_exam.detail: Prochlorperazine and diphenhydramine given at outside ED; discharged on azithromycin

history_exam.label: Medications given by PCP
history_exam.detail: Trimethobenzamide and intravenous fluids administered by PCP

history_exam.label: Empiric hospital antibiotics
history_exam.detail: Vancomycin, ceftriaxone and metronidazole started empirically on admission

history_exam.label: Definitive hospital antibiotics
history_exam.detail: Antibiotics changed to nafcillin and rifampin when cultures identified MSSA; continued for 4 weeks

history_exam.label: Anticoagulation
history_exam.detail: Patient was anticoagulated during admission; later maintained on coumadin pending evaluation for coagulopathy

history_exam.label: Medication allergy history
history_exam.detail: No medication allergies reported; amoxicillin previously caused vomiting

history_exam.label: Household/family
history_exam.detail: Lives with husband, two young children and two dogs

history_exam.label: Children's recent illness
history_exam.detail: Both children had recently had staphylococcal skin infections

history_exam.label: Travel history
history_exam.detail: Recently traveled to Northern New England with her family

history_exam.label: Trauma/exposure history
history_exam.detail: Reported no trauma, no tick exposure, and no insect bites

history_exam.label: Tobacco/alcohol/drug use
history_exam.detail: Does not smoke, drink alcohol, or use illicit drugs

history_exam.label: Temperature
history_exam.detail: 101.9°F (38.8°C) on presentation

history_exam.label: Pulse
history_exam.detail: 100 beats per minute

history_exam.label: Blood pressure
history_exam.detail: 133/66 mm Hg

history_exam.label: Respiratory rate
history_exam.detail: 18 breaths per minute

history_exam.label: Oxygen saturation
history_exam.detail: 96% on ambient air

history_exam.label: General appearance and mentation
history_exam.detail: Appeared uncomfortable, lying in bed with eyes closed, easily roused, oriented, and answered questions appropriately

history_exam.label: Pupils
history_exam.detail: Pupils 4.5 mm bilaterally and constricted to 3 mm with illumination (reactive)

history_exam.label: Right facial swelling
history_exam.detail: Mild facial swelling of the right cheek

history_exam.label: Periorbital edema
history_exam.detail: Mild periorbital edema on the right

history_exam.label: Periorbital erythema and proptosis
history_exam.detail: Erythema and 3–4 mm proptosis of the right eye compared to the left

history_exam.label: Chemosis and ptosis
history_exam.detail: Chemosis and ptosis present on the right

history_exam.label: Extraocular movements (left)
history_exam.detail: Extraocular movements of the left eye normal

history_exam.label: Extraocular movements (right)
history_exam.detail: Right eye: limited medial adduction; no lateral, superior or inferior motion

history_exam.label: Periorbital tenderness
history_exam.detail: Mild tenderness periorbitally on the right

history_exam.label: Forehead lesion
history_exam.detail: Small crusted papule on forehead between the eyebrows

history_exam.label: Fundoscopic exam
history_exam.detail: Fundoscopic examination was normal

history_exam.label: Tympanic membranes
history_exam.detail: Tympanic membranes were normal

history_exam.label: Neck exam
history_exam.detail: Neck stiffness present

history_exam.label: Cervical lymph nodes
history_exam.detail: No cervical lymphadenopathy

history_exam.label: Other systems
history_exam.detail: Remainder of physical examination was normal

investigations.label: Complete blood count (CBC)
investigations.detail: WBC 18,600 /mm3 (93% neutrophils, 3% lymphocytes, 4% monocytes), hematocrit 33.6%, platelets 216,000 /mm3

investigations.label: Serum chemistries and liver function tests
investigations.detail: Serum chemistries and liver function tests reported as normal

investigations.label: D-dimer
investigations.detail: D-dimer 1029 ng/mL (reference <500 ng/mL)

investigations.label: Pregnancy test
investigations.detail: Pregnancy test negative

investigations.label: CSF analysis (glucose/protein/cells)
investigations.detail: CSF glucose 69 mg/dL, protein 180 mg/dL; Tube 1: 1575 RBC/mm3 and 1130 WBC/mm3 (83% neutrophils, 17% monocytes); Tube 4: 195 RBC/mm3 and 3000 WBC/mm3 (82% neutrophils, 18% lymphocytes)

investigations.label: Gram stain of CSF cytospin
investigations.detail: Gram stain of CSF cytospin: abundant polymorphonuclear leukocytes and no organisms visualized

investigations.label: Blood and CSF cultures
investigations.detail: Blood and CSF cultures (day of admission) grew methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus (MSSA)

investigations.label: Chest CT (PE protocol) at outside ED
investigations.detail: Chest CT showed two pulmonary nodules on the right and a left lower lobe abnormality thought to be inflammatory process or infarct

investigations.label: Chest CT during admission
investigations.detail: CT chest showed left upper lobe cavitary lesion and multiple small scattered lesions consistent with septic pulmonary emboli

investigations.label: Brain MRI
investigations.detail: MRI of the brain suggestive of thromboses and dural enhancement consistent with meningitis

investigations.label: CT angiography of the head
investigations.detail: CT angiography revealed thrombosis of both cavernous sinuses, both superior ophthalmic veins (complete on right, partial on left), dorsal clival venous plexus, right sigmoid sinus, right internal jugular vein, and likely bilateral sphenoparietal sinuses

investigations.label: CT scan of the neck
investigations.detail: CT neck showed some soft tissue swelling but no evidence of abscess

investigations.label: Transthoracic echocardiography
investigations.detail: Transthoracic echocardiogram showed no vegetations

investigations.label: Lumbar puncture
investigations.detail: Lumbar puncture performed with CSF analysis and cytospin gram stain

investigations.label: Gram stain of aerobic blood culture
investigations.detail: Gram stain of aerobic blood culture demonstrated gram-positive cocci consistent with Staphylococcus (culture confirmed MSSA)

investigations.label: Empiric antimicrobial therapy initiated
investigations.detail: Empiric IV vancomycin, ceftriaxone and metronidazole started on admission

investigations.label: Definitive antimicrobial therapy and duration
investigations.detail: Therapy changed to nafcillin and rifampin for methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus and continued for 4 weeks

investigations.label: Anticoagulation during admission
investigations.detail: Anticoagulation was initiated during admission; patient continued on coumadin post-discharge pending coagulopathy evaluation

investigations.label: Follow-up CT angiography (~10 months)
investigations.detail: CT angiography 10 months after admission: non-significant opacification in bilateral cavernous sinuses, normal caliber superior ophthalmic veins bilaterally, stable persistent thrombosis of right sigmoid sinus, jugular sinus and internal jugular vein; transverse sinus enhanced normally

investigations.label: Clinical course (fever and ocular improvement)
investigations.detail: Fevers resolved during admission; slow but significant improvement in ptosis, proptosis and eye movement; near-complete resolution by ~3 months except intermittent headaches at ~9 months
